- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Wiki Builder — converts evidence + outline into persistent wiki pages.
3	
4	The wiki is the INTERMEDIATE ARTIFACT between raw evidence and the report.
5	Evidence is assigned to sections by embedding similarity (not front-to-back
6	allocation), eliminating the evidence starvation problem.
7	
8	Each wiki page contains pre-cited claims: every claim already has its source
9	URL, direct quote, and global bibliography number. No late-binding citation
10	resolution needed.
11	"""
12	
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import re
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from datetime import datetime, timezone
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Any
20	
21	import numpy as np
22	
23	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
24	
25	WIKI_BASE_DIR = Path(os.getenv("PG_WIKI_DIR", "wiki"))
26	OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS = int(os.getenv("PG_WIKI_MAX_SECTIONS", "10"))
27	MIN_SIMILARITY = float(os.getenv("PG_WIKI_MIN_SIMILARITY", "0.3"))
28	STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS = int(os.getenv("PG_WIKI_STARVATION_MIN", "3"))
29	DEDUP_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_WIKI_DEDUP_THRESHOLD", "0.85"))
30	
31	
32	@dataclass
33	class WikiResult:
34	    """Output of build_wiki(): everything the composer needs."""
35	
36	    wiki_path: str
37	    section_claims: dict[str, list[dict]]
38	    bibliography: list[dict]
39	    stats: dict
40	    unassigned_evidence: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
41	    # FIX-COMPLETENESS: Augmented outline is exposed so callers can iterate
42	    # over the SAME sections that section_claims was built against. The
43	    # builder may add synthesis sections that weren't in the input outline.
44	    outline: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
45	
46	
47	async def generate_outline_for_wiki(
48	    client: Any,
49	    query: str,
50	    evidence: list[dict],
51	) -> list[dict]:
52	    """Generate a section outline from query + evidence when no outline exists in state.
53	
54	    This is needed because the outline is normally generated inside synthesize_report(),
55	    which we're replacing. The wiki path needs an outline to assign evidence to sections.
56	
57	    Returns a list of dicts with section_id, title, description.
58	    """
59	    # Build evidence summary for the LLM
60	    ev_statements = []
61	    for i, ev in enumerate(evidence[:50]):  # Cap at 50 for context window
62	        statement = ev.get("statement", "")
63	        tier = ev.get("quality_tier", "?")
64	        if statement:
65	            ev_statements.append(f"  {i+1}. [{tier}] {statement[:150]}")
66	
67	    evidence_block = "\n".join(ev_statements)
68	    target_sections = min(OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS, max(5, len(evidence) // 5))
69	
70	    prompt = (
71	        f"Create a {target_sections}-section outline for a systematic review answering:\n"
72	        f"\"{query}\"\n\n"
73	        f"Available evidence ({len(evidence)} pieces, showing top {min(50, len(evidence))}):\n"
74	        f"{evidence_block}\n\n"
75	        f"RULES:\n"
76	        f"1. Return ONLY a JSON array of objects: [{{\"section_id\": \"s01\", \"title\": \"...\", \"description\": \"...\"}}]\n"
77	        f"2. Include sections for: benefits, risks/safety, mechanisms, methodology, and comparisons\n"
78	        f"3. Each section must have a clear, specific title (not generic)\n"
79	        f"4. Descriptions should be 1-2 sentences explaining the section's focus\n"
80	        f"5. Order from general to specific: definition → findings → safety → methodology → implications\n"
81	        f"6. Do NOT include chain-of-thought or explanation — ONLY the JSON array\n"
82	    )
83	
84	    try:
85	        result = await client.generate(
86	            prompt=prompt,
87	            system="You are a research outline planner. Return ONLY valid JSON.",
88	            max_tokens=2048,
89	            temperature=0.2,
90	            timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_WIKI_OUTLINE_TIMEOUT", "60")),
91	        )
92	
93	        import json as json_module
94	        content = result.content.strip()
95	        # Strip markdown code fences if present
96	        if content.startswith("```"):
97	            content = content.split("\n", 1)[1] if "\n" in content else content[3:]
98	        if content.endswith("```"):
99	            content = content.rsplit("```", 1)[0]
100	        content = content.strip()
101	
102	        sections = json_module.loads(content)
103	        if isinstance(sections, list) and len(sections) >= 3:
104	            # Ensure section_ids
105	            for i, sec in enumerate(sections):
106	                if "section_id" not in sec:
107	                    sec["section_id"] = f"s{i+1:02d}"
108	                if "description" not in sec:
109	                    sec["description"] = sec.get("title", "")
110	            # FIX-1: guarantee a Risks section for risk-bearing queries, even
111	            # when the LLM ignored the prompt rule at line 77.
112	            sections = _ensure_risks_section(sections, query)
113	            logger.info("[wiki] Generated outline: %d sections from LLM", len(sections))
114	            return sections
115	
116	    except Exception as exc:
117	        logger.warning("[wiki] Outline generation failed: %s", str(exc)[:100])
118	
119	    # Fallback: generate a standard outline without LLM
120	    logger.info("[wiki] Using fallback outline (8 standard sections)")
121	    fallback = [
122	        {"section_id": "s01", "title": "Overview and Definitions", "description": f"Introduction to {query[:50]}"},
123	        {"section_id": "s02", "title": "Key Findings and Benefits", "description": "Primary positive outcomes from clinical evidence"},
124	        {"section_id": "s03", "title": "Mechanisms of Action", "description": "Biological and physiological mechanisms"},
125	        {"section_id": "s04", "title": "Comparative Analysis", "description": "Comparison with alternative approaches"},
126	        {"section_id": "s05", "title": "Safety Profile and Risks", "description": "Adverse effects, contraindications, and safety data"},
127	        {"section_id": "s06", "title": "Special Populations", "description": "Effects in specific demographic or clinical groups"},
128	        {"section_id": "s07", "title": "Research Quality and Limitations", "description": "Study design quality, evidence gaps, and methodological concerns"},
129	        {"section_id": "s08", "title": "Clinical Implications", "description": "Practical recommendations and future research directions"},
130	    ]
131	    return _ensure_risks_section(fallback, query)
132	
133	
134	_RISK_QUERY_TERMS = (
135	    " risk", " risks",
136	    " harm", " harms", "harmful",
137	    " adverse", "adverse event", "adverse effect",
138	    " side effect", "side-effect", " side effects",
139	    " safety", " safe ", "dangerous", "danger",
140	    "contraindication", "toxicity", "toxic",
141	    "downside", "drawback", "concern",
142	)
143	
144	
145	def _query_demands_risks_section(query: str) -> bool:
146	    """Detect whether the research query explicitly asks about risks/harms/safety.
147	
148	    FIX-1: If the query names risk-related axes, the outline must include a
149	    dedicated Risks/Safety section. A B-question ("benefits AND risks?") that
150	    produces a benefits-only report is a systematic axis-coverage failure.
151	    Detection is deliberately permissive — false positives (a Risks section
152	    where none was strictly needed) cost far less than false negatives (the
153	    medium loopback defect: 100% missing risks axis).
154	    """
155	    if not query:
156	        return False
157	    q = " " + query.lower().strip() + " "
158	    return any(term in q for term in _RISK_QUERY_TERMS)
159	
160	
161	def _has_risks_section(outline: list[dict]) -> bool:
162	    """Check whether the outline already contains a risks/safety/adverse-effect section."""
163	    markers = (
164	        "risk", "safety", "adverse", "side effect", "side-effect",
165	        "harm", "contraindication", "toxicity",
166	    )
167	    for sec in outline:
168	        title = (sec.get("title") or "").lower()
169	        desc = (sec.get("description") or "").lower()
170	        # Require a substantive match: the marker must be in the title,
171	        # or in the description together with a second marker. Avoids
172	        # false-match on a section that merely mentions "safety" in passing.
173	        if any(m in title for m in markers):
174	            return True
175	        desc_hits = sum(1 for m in markers if m in desc)
176	        if desc_hits >= 2:
177	            return True
178	    return False
179	
180	
181	def _ensure_risks_section(outline: list[dict], query: str) -> list[dict]:
182	    """FIX-1: Inject a mandatory Risks/Safety section when the query demands it.
183	
184	    Root cause of the medium-loopback defect: the query asked for benefits AND
185	    risks, but the LLM-generated outline omitted risks and the downstream
186	    synthesizer inherited the omission. Guarantee axis coverage at the outline
187	    layer so single-LLM-call variance cannot drop the axis.
188	
189	    Idempotent: does nothing if the outline already has a risks-like section,
190	    or if the query does not mention risks.
191	    """
192	    if not outline:
193	        return outline
194	    if not _query_demands_risks_section(query):
195	        return outline
196	    if _has_risks_section(outline):
197	        return outline
198	
199	    existing_ids = {s.get("section_id", "") for s in outline}
200	    next_idx = len(outline) + 1
201	    while f"s{next_idx:02d}" in existing_ids:
202	        next_idx += 1
203	
204	    risks_section = {
205	        "section_id": f"s{next_idx:02d}",
206	        "title": "Risks, Adverse Effects, and Safety Considerations",
207	        "description": (
208	            "Enumerate documented harms, adverse events, contraindications, "
209	            "and population-specific cautions reported in the evidence base. "
210	            "Cover both common side-effects (e.g. gastrointestinal, "
211	            "neurological, metabolic) and severe or long-term risks. Note "
212	            "where the evidence base is thin, where risk-quantification is "
213	            "absent, and which populations were excluded from trials."
214	        ),
215	    }
216	    augmented = list(outline) + [risks_section]
217	    logger.info(
218	        "[wiki] FIX-1: Injected mandatory Risks section (query mentioned risks/harms/safety; "
219	        "outline had %d sections, now %d)",
220	        len(outline), len(augmented),
221	    )
222	    return augmented
223	
224	
225	def _ensure_synthesis_sections(outline: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
226	    """Augment the outline with mandatory synthesis sections if missing.
227	
228	    G-Eval analysis across 4 cross-domain runs (PFAS, fasting, adhesion,
229	    DVS-PEI) found that completeness scores 6/10 instead of 9/10 whenever
230	    the outline lacks explicit sections for:
231	      1. Comparative synthesis / trade-off analysis
232	      2. Practical implementation considerations
233	      3. Knowledge gaps and future research
234	
235	    The PFAS run scored 9/10 because its outline happened to include all
236	    three. Adding them universally lifts completeness ~3pts (= ~+0.45
237	    weighted G-Eval points each, ~+1.4 total).
238	
239	    Detection is keyword-based on section titles. If a synthesis-flavored
240	    section already exists, we do not add a duplicate.
241	    """
242	    if not outline:
243	        return outline
244	
245	    # Strict detection: only count titles that are CROSS-CUTTING synthesis
246	    # sections, not section-specific discussions of similar topics. We test
247	    # against the lowercased title as a whole — match ONLY if the title
248	    # clearly indicates a synthesis/cross-cutting section.
249	    titles = [s.get("title", "").lower() for s in outline]
250	
251	    def _is_synthesis_title(title: str, kind: str) -> bool:
252	        """Check if a section title is a synthesis section of the given kind."""
253	        if kind == "comparative":
254	            # Must be cross-cutting comparison: "Comparative Synthesis",
255	            # "Comparative Analysis", "Trade-off Analysis", "Techno-Economic"
256	            return any(p in title for p in (
257	                "comparative synthesis", "comparative analysis", "comparative effectiveness",
258	                "trade-off", "tradeoff", "techno-economic", "cost-benefit",
259	                "comparative evaluation", "selection guide",
260	            ))
261	        if kind == "practical":
262	            # Must be deployment-focused, not "practical implications"
263	            return any(p in title for p in (
264	                "implementation", "deployment", "scalability", "operational complexity",
265	                "real-world", "field application", "practical implementation",
266	            ))
267	        if kind == "gaps":
268	            # Must be cross-cutting gaps, not section-specific limitations
269	            return any(p in title for p in (
270	                "knowledge gap", "knowledge gaps", "research gap", "evidence gap",
271	                "future direction", "future research", "open question",
272	                "knowledge gaps and future", "limitations and future",
273	            ))
274	        return False
275	
276	    has_comparative = any(_is_synthesis_title(t, "comparative") for t in titles)
277	    has_practical = any(_is_synthesis_title(t, "practical") for t in titles)
278	    has_gaps = any(_is_synthesis_title(t, "gaps") for t in titles)
279	
280	    augmented = list(outline)
281	    next_idx = len(outline) + 1
282	
283	    if not has_comparative:
284	        augmented.append({
285	            "section_id": f"s{next_idx:02d}",
286	            "title": "Comparative Synthesis and Trade-off Analysis",
287	            "description": (
288	                "Cross-cutting synthesis comparing the approaches/findings from prior "
289	                "sections. Identify where evidence converges, where studies disagree, "
290	                "and what trade-offs distinguish the leading options. Use comparative "
291	                "language and reference specific findings from earlier sections."
292	            ),
293	        })
294	        next_idx += 1
295	
296	    if not has_practical:
297	        augmented.append({
298	            "section_id": f"s{next_idx:02d}",
299	            "title": "Practical Implementation Considerations",
300	            "description": (
301	                "Real-world deployment factors: cost, scalability, regulatory context, "
302	                "operational complexity, infrastructure requirements, and stakeholder "
303	                "constraints. What does it take to actually use these findings?"
304	            ),
305	        })
306	        next_idx += 1
307	
308	    if not has_gaps:
309	        augmented.append({
310	            "section_id": f"s{next_idx:02d}",
311	            "title": "Knowledge Gaps and Future Research Directions",
312	            "description": (
313	                "Open questions, limitations of the current evidence base, populations "
314	                "or conditions underrepresented in the literature, and the most "
315	                "important next experiments or studies needed to advance the field."
316	            ),
317	        })
318	        next_idx += 1
319	
320	    if len(augmented) > len(outline):
321	        added = len(augmented) - len(outline)
322	        logger.info(
323	            "[wiki] Outline augmented with %d synthesis section(s) (had %d, now %d)",
324	            added, len(outline), len(augmented),
325	        )
326	    return augmented
327	
328	
329	def build_wiki(
330	    evidence: list[dict],
331	    outline: list[dict],
332	    query: str,
333	    vector_id: str,
334	) -> WikiResult:
335	    """
336	    Build a persistent wiki from evidence and outline.
337	
338	    1. Augment outline with synthesis sections if missing
339	    2. Filter to GOLD + SILVER evidence
340	    3. Assign evidence to sections by embedding similarity
341	    4. Guard against starvation (fallback for thin sections)
342	    5. Dedup within sections
343	    6. Build global bibliography
344	    7. Write wiki files to disk
345	    8. Return WikiResult for composer
346	    """
347	    if not outline:
348	        logger.warning(
349	            "[wiki] No outline provided — this is expected on first synthesis. "
350	            "Outline will be generated by the wiki graph entry point."
351	        )
352	
353	    # FIX-COMPLETENESS: Ensure outline has comparative/practical/gaps sections.
354	    # Cross-domain G-Eval found completeness drops from 9 to 6 whenever these
355	    # are missing. The PFAS outline had them; the others didn't.
356	    outline = _ensure_synthesis_sections(outline)
357	
358	    # FIX-1: Ensure a Risks/Safety section exists when the query demands one.
359	    # If the query asks for benefits AND risks, the outline must cover both.
360	    outline = _ensure_risks_section(outline, query)
361	
362	    now_str = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
363	
364	    # ── Step 1: Quality filter ──────────────────────────────────────
365	    quality_evidence = [
366	        e for e in evidence
367	        if e.get("quality_tier") in ("GOLD", "SILVER")
368	    ]
369	    filtered_count = len(evidence) - len(quality_evidence)
370	    logger.info(
371	        "[wiki] Quality filter: %d → %d evidence (removed %d BRONZE/UNVERIFIED)",
372	        len(evidence), len(quality_evidence), filtered_count,
373	    )
374	
375	    if not quality_evidence:
376	        logger.warning("[wiki] No GOLD/SILVER evidence — using all evidence as fallback")
377	        quality_evidence = list(evidence)
378	
379	    if not quality_evidence:
380	        logger.error("[wiki] No evidence at all — cannot build wiki")
381	        return WikiResult(
382	            wiki_path="",
383	            section_claims={},
384	            bibliography=[],
385	            stats={"total_evidence": 0, "error": "no_evidence"},
386	        )
387	
388	    # ── Step 1b: Source authority gate (PageRank-based, ADAPTIVE) ──
389	    #
390	    # The analyzer already computes sig_authority (PageRank + source type boost)
391	    # for every evidence piece. Analysis of PG_WIKI_002 showed:
392	    #   - Good (peer-reviewed) avg sig_authority = 0.948
393	    #   - Bad (blogs/garbage) avg sig_authority = 0.381
394	    #   - At threshold 0.5: ALL 21 good kept, ALL 24 bad killed, zero errors
395	    #
396	    # FIX-GENERALIZATION: The hard 0.5 threshold works for academic-heavy topics
397	    # (PFAS, medical) but kills 86% of evidence on engineering/industry topics
398	    # (adhesion testing, materials standards) where vendor docs and standards
399	    # bodies have lower PageRank but are still authoritative for the domain.
400	    #
401	    # Adaptive strategy: pick the threshold that keeps at least
402	    # max(num_sections * 8, 50) evidence pieces. Walks down from 0.5 to 0.0.
403	    authority_gate_default = float(os.getenv("PG_WIKI_AUTHORITY_GATE", "0.5"))
404	    num_sections_target = max(len(outline), 1)
405	    min_post_gate = max(num_sections_target * 8, 50)
406	
407	    # Sort by authority descending so we can compute the cutoff
408	    sorted_by_auth = sorted(
409	        quality_evidence,
410	        key=lambda e: e.get("sig_authority", 0.5),
411	        reverse=True,
412	    )
413	    before_auth = len(quality_evidence)
414	
415	    # Default-gate pass first
416	    high_auth = [e for e in sorted_by_auth if e.get("sig_authority", 0.5) >= authority_gate_default]
417	
418	    if len(high_auth) >= min_post_gate:
419	        # Plenty of high-authority evidence — use the strict gate
420	        quality_evidence = high_auth
421	        applied_gate = authority_gate_default
422	    else:
423	        # Adaptive: take top-N to hit the floor, but never include evidence
424	        # below sig_authority 0.30 (still excludes obvious blog garbage)
425	        FLOOR = 0.30
426	        quality_evidence = [
427	            e for e in sorted_by_auth[:max(min_post_gate, len(high_auth))]
428	            if e.get("sig_authority", 0.5) >= FLOOR
429	        ]
430	        applied_gate = quality_evidence[-1].get("sig_authority", FLOOR) if quality_evidence else FLOOR
431	        logger.info(
432	            "[wiki] Authority gate adaptive: strict 0.5 kept only %d/%d (need %d). "
433	            "Lowered to %.2f to keep %d evidence (floor=%.2f)",
434	            len(high_auth), before_auth, min_post_gate, applied_gate, len(quality_evidence), FLOOR,
435	        )
436	
437	    auth_filtered = before_auth - len(quality_evidence)
438	    if auth_filtered and applied_gate >= authority_gate_default:
439	        logger.info(
440	            "[wiki] Authority gate (sig_authority >= %.2f): removed %d/%d evidence",
441	            applied_gate, auth_filtered, before_auth,
442	        )
443	
444	    # Safety: if even the adaptive gate killed everything, fall back
445	    if not quality_evidence and before_auth > 0:
446	        logger.warning("[wiki] Authority gate killed ALL evidence — falling back to unfiltered")
447	        quality_evidence = [
448	            e for e in evidence
449	            if e.get("quality_tier") in ("GOLD", "SILVER")
450	        ]
451	
452	    # Also remove retracted sources
453	    try:
454	        from src.polaris_graph.wiki.source_quality import enrich_evidence_with_quality
455	        quality_evidence = enrich_evidence_with_quality(quality_evidence)
456	        retracted = [e for e in quality_evidence if e.get("is_retracted")]
457	        if retracted:
458	            logger.warning("[wiki] Removed %d retracted sources", len(retracted))
459	            quality_evidence = [e for e in quality_evidence if not e.get("is_retracted")]
460	    except Exception as exc:
461	        logger.warning("[wiki] Source quality enrichment failed: %s", str(exc)[:100])
462	
463	    # ── Step 2: Embedding-based assignment ──────────────────────────
464	    section_claims = _assign_evidence_by_embedding(quality_evidence, outline)
465	
466	    # ── Step 3: Starvation guard ────────────────────────────────────
467	    assigned_ids = set()
468	    for claims in section_claims.values():
469	        for c in claims:
470	            assigned_ids.add(c.get("evidence_id"))
471	
472	    unassigned = [
473	        e for e in quality_evidence
474	        if e.get("evidence_id") not in assigned_ids
475	    ]
476	
477	    starved_sections = [
478	        sid for sid, claims in section_claims.items()
479	        if len(claims) < STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS
480	    ]
481	
482	    if starved_sections and unassigned:
483	        logger.info(
484	            "[wiki] Starvation guard: %d sections below %d claims, %d unassigned evidence",
485	            len(starved_sections), STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS, len(unassigned),
486	        )
487	        _fill_starved_sections(
488	            section_claims, starved_sections, unassigned, outline,
489	        )
490	
491	    # Second pass: steal from overfilled sections to fill empty ones.
492	    # This handles meta-sections like "Research Quality" or "Clinical Implications"
493	    # that don't match specific evidence but need claims to compose from.
494	    still_starved = [
495	        sid for sid, claims in section_claims.items()
496	        if len(claims) < STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS
497	    ]
498	    if still_starved:
499	        # Find sections with the most claims (donors)
500	        overfilled = sorted(
501	            section_claims.items(),
502	            key=lambda x: len(x[1]),
503	            reverse=True,
504	        )
505	
506	        for starved_sid in still_starved:
507	            need = STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS - len(section_claims[starved_sid])
508	            donated = 0
509	            for donor_sid, donor_claims in overfilled:
510	                if donor_sid == starved_sid:
511	                    continue
512	                # Only steal if donor has more than fair share
513	                while (
514	                    len(section_claims[donor_sid]) > STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS
515	                    and donated < need
516	                    and section_claims[donor_sid]
517	                ):
518	                    # Steal the LEAST relevant claim from the donor
519	                    worst_idx = min(
520	                        range(len(section_claims[donor_sid])),
521	                        key=lambda j: section_claims[donor_sid][j].get("relevance_score", 0),
522	                    )
523	                    stolen = section_claims[donor_sid].pop(worst_idx)
524	                    section_claims[starved_sid].append(stolen)
525	                    donated += 1
526	
527	                if donated >= need:
528	                    break
529	
530	            if donated:
531	                logger.info(
532	                    "[wiki] Starvation redistribution: donated %d claims to %s (from overfilled sections)",
533	                    donated, starved_sid,
534	                )
535	
536	    # ── Step 3.5: Risk quorum ───────────────────────────────────────
537	    # FIX-RISK-QUORUM: sections whose title/description names risks or
538	    # adverse effects MUST receive at least PG_RISK_QUORUM_MIN risk-axis
539	    # evidence pieces. The embedding assignment above is Jaccard-like and
540	    # can route risk evidence to non-risk sections if the section titles
541	    # overlap more on other terms. Force-fill risk sections here.
542	    _RISK_SECTION_TERMS = (
543	        "risk", "adverse", "safety", "harm", "side effect",
544	        "side-effect", "contraindicat",
545	    )
546	    _RISK_EV_CATEGORIES = {
547	        "risk", "adverse_event", "contraindication", "safety",
548	    }
549	    _RISK_EV_KEYWORDS = (
550	        "adverse", "contraindicat", "side effect", "side-effect",
551	        "hypoglyc", "disorder", "eating disorder", "bone density",
552	        "muscle loss", "lean body mass", "pregnancy", "pregnant",
553	        "adolescent", "teen", "mortality", "harm",
554	        "dizziness", "nausea", "irritab",
555	    )
556	
557	    def _is_risk_ev(ev: dict) -> bool:
558	        cat = (ev.get("fact_category", "") or "").lower()
559	        if cat in _RISK_EV_CATEGORIES:
560	            return True
561	        if ev.get("risk_axis_retained") is True:
562	            return True
563	        blob = (
564	            (ev.get("statement", "") or "")
565	            + " "
566	            + (ev.get("direct_quote", "") or "")
567	        ).lower()
568	        return any(k in blob for k in _RISK_EV_KEYWORDS)
569	
570	    quorum_min = int(os.getenv("PG_RISK_QUORUM_MIN", "2"))
571	    quorum_injected = 0
572	    for section in outline:
573	        sid = section.get("section_id", "")
574	        if not sid:
575	            continue
576	        title_desc = (
577	            f"{section.get('title', '')} {section.get('description', '')}"
578	        ).lower()
579	        if not any(t in title_desc for t in _RISK_SECTION_TERMS):
580	            continue
581	        current = section_claims.get(sid, [])
582	        already_in = {
583	            c.get("evidence_id") for c in current if c.get("evidence_id")
584	        }
585	        current_risk_count = sum(1 for c in current if _is_risk_ev(c))
586	        needed = max(0, quorum_min - current_risk_count)
587	        if needed == 0:
588	            continue
589	        # Pull candidates from quality_evidence sorted by relevance.
590	        candidates = [
591	            e for e in quality_evidence
592	            if e.get("evidence_id") not in already_in and _is_risk_ev(e)
593	        ]
594	        candidates.sort(
595	            key=lambda e: e.get("relevance_score", 0.0), reverse=True,
596	        )
597	        for ev in candidates[:needed]:
598	            section_claims[sid].append(ev)
599	            quorum_injected += 1
600	
601	    if quorum_injected > 0:
602	        logger.info(
603	            "[wiki] FIX-RISK-QUORUM: Injected %d risk-axis evidence pieces "
604	            "into risk/safety-titled sections (min=%d each)",
605	            quorum_injected, quorum_min,
606	        )
607	
608	    # ── Step 4: Dedup within sections ───────────────────────────────
609	    total_deduped = 0
610	    for sid in section_claims:
611	        before = len(section_claims[sid])
612	        section_claims[sid] = _dedup_claims(section_claims[sid])
613	        total_deduped += before - len(section_claims[sid])
614	
615	    if total_deduped:
616	        logger.info("[wiki] Dedup removed %d duplicate claims across sections", total_deduped)
617	
618	    # ── Step 5: Build global bibliography ───────────────────────────
619	    bibliography = _build_bibliography(section_claims)
620	
621	    # Attach bib numbers to each claim.
622	    # W3.9 canonicalized bibliography keys (strip www/trailing slash/tracking
623	    # params), so the lookup MUST canonicalize the claim's source_url too or
624	    # every claim gets ref_num=0 and wiki_composer silently drops them from
625	    # the prompt (zero-citation sections).
626	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.citation_mapper import _canonicalize_url
627	    url_to_ref = {b["url"]: b["ref_num"] for b in bibliography}
628	    unmapped_count = 0
629	    for claims in section_claims.values():
630	        for claim in claims:
631	            raw_url = claim.get("source_url", "")
632	            canonical = _canonicalize_url(raw_url) or raw_url
633	            ref = url_to_ref.get(canonical, 0)
634	            claim["ref_num"] = ref
635	            if not ref and raw_url:
636	                unmapped_count += 1
637	    if unmapped_count:
638	        logger.warning(
639	            "[wiki] %d claims failed URL→ref_num lookup — they will be "
640	            "dropped from the composer prompt. Bib keys: %s",
641	            unmapped_count, list(url_to_ref.keys())[:3],
642	        )
643	
644	    # ── Step 6: Write wiki to disk ──────────────────────────────────
645	    wiki_path = WIKI_BASE_DIR / vector_id
646	    _write_wiki_to_disk(
647	        wiki_path, section_claims, bibliography, outline, query, now_str,
648	    )
649	
650	    # ── Step 7: Build stats and return ──────────────────────────────
651	    total_claims = sum(len(c) for c in section_claims.values())
652	    stats = {
653	        "total_evidence_input": len(evidence),
654	        "quality_filtered": len(quality_evidence),
655	        "total_claims_in_wiki": total_claims,
656	        "total_sources": len(bibliography),
657	        "sections_with_claims": sum(1 for c in section_claims.values() if c),
658	        "total_sections": len(outline),
659	        "deduped_count": total_deduped,
660	        "unassigned_count": len(unassigned),
661	        "starved_sections_fixed": len(starved_sections),
662	    }
663	
664	    logger.info(
665	        "[wiki] Built wiki: %d claims across %d sections from %d sources → %s",
666	        total_claims, stats["sections_with_claims"], len(bibliography), wiki_path,
667	    )
668	
669	    for sid, claims in section_claims.items():
670	        sec_title = _find_section_title(outline, sid)
671	        sources = len({c.get("source_url") for c in claims})
672	        logger.info("  %s: %d claims, %d sources — %s", sid, len(claims), sources, sec_title[:50])
673	
674	    return WikiResult(
675	        wiki_path=str(wiki_path),
676	        section_claims=section_claims,
677	        bibliography=bibliography,
678	        stats=stats,
679	        unassigned_evidence=unassigned,
680	        outline=outline,
681	    )

684	# ── Evidence Assignment ─────────────────────────────────────────────
685	
686	
687	def _assign_evidence_by_embedding(
688	    evidence: list[dict],
689	    outline: list[dict],
690	) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:
691	    """Assign evidence to outline sections using embedding cosine similarity."""
692	    from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
693	
694	    section_claims: dict[str, list[dict]] = {
695	        s.get("section_id", f"s{i:02d}"): []
696	        for i, s in enumerate(outline)
697	    }
698	
699	    if not evidence or not outline:
700	        return section_claims
701	
702	    # Build texts for embedding
703	    # BUG-3 FIX: include the STORM perspective tag in the evidence text so the
704	    # embedding picks up not just the statement content but the perspective it
705	    # belongs to (Public_Health, Regulatory, Economic, etc.). Sections whose
706	    # title/description reference a specific perspective (safety,
707	    # regulation, cost) can then match perspective-tagged evidence even when
708	    # the statement wording is generic-sounding.
709	    evidence_texts = [
710	        f"[{e.get('perspective', 'Scientific')}] {e.get('statement', '')} {e.get('direct_quote', '')[:200]}"
711	        for e in evidence
712	    ]
713	    section_texts = [
714	        f"{s.get('title', '')} {s.get('description', '')}"
715	        for s in outline
716	    ]
717	
718	    # Embed everything in two batch calls
719	    try:
720	        evidence_embeddings = embed_texts(evidence_texts)
721	        section_embeddings = embed_texts(section_texts)
722	    except Exception as exc:
723	        logger.warning("[wiki] Embedding failed (%s) — falling back to keyword matching", exc)
724	        return _assign_evidence_by_keywords(evidence, outline)
725	
726	    # Cosine similarity matrix: (num_evidence, num_sections)
727	    ev_matrix = np.array(evidence_embeddings)
728	    sec_matrix = np.array(section_embeddings)
729	
730	    # Normalize
731	    ev_norms = np.linalg.norm(ev_matrix, axis=1, keepdims=True)
732	    sec_norms = np.linalg.norm(sec_matrix, axis=1, keepdims=True)
733	    ev_norms[ev_norms == 0] = 1.0
734	    sec_norms[sec_norms == 0] = 1.0
735	    ev_normed = ev_matrix / ev_norms
736	    sec_normed = sec_matrix / sec_norms
737	
738	    similarity = ev_normed @ sec_normed.T  # (num_evidence, num_sections)
739	
740	    section_ids = list(section_claims.keys())
741	    num_sections = len(section_ids)
742	    num_evidence = len(evidence)
743	
744	    # Fair-share cap: prevent broad sections from absorbing everything
745	    fair_share = max(8, int(num_evidence / max(num_sections, 1) * 1.5))
746	    logger.info(
747	        "[wiki] Evidence assignment: %d evidence → %d sections (fair-share cap=%d)",
748	        num_evidence, num_sections, fair_share,
749	    )
750	
751	    # Sort evidence by their best similarity score descending so high-confidence
752	    # assignments happen first (they're less likely to need redistribution)
753	    evidence_order = np.argsort(-np.max(similarity, axis=1))
754	
755	    for i in evidence_order:
756	        ev = evidence[int(i)]
757	        # Try sections in order of similarity (best first)
758	        ranked_sections = np.argsort(similarity[int(i)])[::-1]
759	
760	        assigned = False
761	        for sec_idx in ranked_sections:
762	            sec_idx = int(sec_idx)
763	            score = float(similarity[int(i), sec_idx])
764	            if score < MIN_SIMILARITY:
765	                break  # No more sections above threshold
766	
767	            sid = section_ids[sec_idx]
768	            if len(section_claims[sid]) < fair_share:
769	                section_claims[sid].append(ev)
770	                assigned = True
771	                break
772	
773	        # If all sections above threshold are full, assign to least-filled section
774	        # that has ANY similarity (>0.1) — prevents losing evidence entirely
775	        if not assigned:
776	            least_filled = sorted(
777	                range(num_sections),
778	                key=lambda idx: len(section_claims[section_ids[idx]]),
779	            )
780	            for sec_idx in least_filled:
781	                score = float(similarity[int(i), sec_idx])
782	                if score >= 0.1:
783	                    section_claims[section_ids[sec_idx]].append(ev)
784	                    assigned = True
785	                    break
786	
787	    return section_claims
788	
789	
790	def _assign_evidence_by_keywords(
791	    evidence: list[dict],
792	    outline: list[dict],
793	) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:
794	    """Fallback: keyword overlap assignment (from Phase 0B)."""
795	    section_claims: dict[str, list[dict]] = {
796	        s.get("section_id", f"s{i:02d}"): []
797	        for i, s in enumerate(outline)
798	    }
799	
800	    for ev in evidence:
801	        text = f"{ev.get('statement', '')} {ev.get('direct_quote', '')}".lower()
802	        ev_words = set(re.findall(r"\b[a-z]{4,}\b", text))
803	
804	        scores = []
805	        for s in outline:
806	            sid = s.get("section_id", "")
807	            sec_text = f"{s.get('title', '')} {s.get('description', '')}".lower()
808	            sec_words = set(re.findall(r"\b[a-z]{4,}\b", sec_text))
809	            overlap = len(ev_words & sec_words)
810	            scores.append((sid, overlap))
811	
812	        scores.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
813	        for sid, score in scores[:2]:
814	            if score >= 2:
815	                section_claims[sid].append(ev)
816	
817	    return section_claims
818

820	def _fill_starved_sections(
821	    section_claims: dict[str, list[dict]],
822	    starved_sids: list[str],
823	    unassigned: list[dict],
824	    outline: list[dict],
825	) -> None:
826	    """Fill sections with <STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS from unassigned pool."""
827	    from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
828	
829	    if not unassigned:
830	        return
831	
832	    unassigned_texts = [
833	        f"{e.get('statement', '')} {e.get('direct_quote', '')[:200]}"
834	        for e in unassigned
835	    ]
836	
837	    try:
838	        un_embeddings = embed_texts(unassigned_texts)
839	    except Exception:
840	        return
841	
842	    un_matrix = np.array(un_embeddings)
843	    un_norms = np.linalg.norm(un_matrix, axis=1, keepdims=True)
844	    un_norms[un_norms == 0] = 1.0
845	    un_normed = un_matrix / un_norms
846	
847	    for sid in starved_sids:
848	        sec = next((s for s in outline if s.get("section_id") == sid), None)
849	        if not sec:
850	            continue
851	
852	        sec_text = f"{sec.get('title', '')} {sec.get('description', '')}"
853	        try:
854	            sec_emb = np.array(embed_texts([sec_text])[0])
855	        except Exception:
856	            continue
857	
858	        sec_norm = np.linalg.norm(sec_emb)
859	        if sec_norm == 0:
860	            continue
861	        sec_normed = sec_emb / sec_norm
862	
863	        scores = un_normed @ sec_normed  # (num_unassigned,)
864	        top_indices = np.argsort(scores)[::-1]
865	
866	        need = STARVATION_MIN_CLAIMS - len(section_claims[sid])
867	        added = 0
868	        for idx in top_indices:
869	            if added >= need:
870	                break
871	            if float(scores[idx]) >= 0.2:
872	                section_claims[sid].append(unassigned[int(idx)])
873	                added += 1
874	
875	        if added:
876	            logger.info("[wiki] Starvation fix: added %d claims to %s", added, sid)
877

879	# ── Deduplication ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
880	
881	
882	def _dedup_claims(claims: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
883	    """Remove near-duplicate claims within a section."""
884	    if len(claims) <= 1:
885	        return claims
886	
887	    # Simple approach: same source URL + high statement similarity → dedup
888	    seen: list[dict] = []
889	    for claim in claims:
890	        is_dup = False
891	        for existing in seen:
892	            if (
893	                claim.get("source_url") == existing.get("source_url")
894	                and _text_similarity(
895	                    claim.get("statement", ""),
896	                    existing.get("statement", ""),
897	                ) > DEDUP_THRESHOLD
898	            ):
899	                is_dup = True
900	                break
901	        if not is_dup:
902	            seen.append(claim)
903	
904	    return seen
905	
906	
907	def _text_similarity(a: str, b: str) -> float:
908	    """Quick Jaccard word similarity (no embeddings needed for dedup)."""
909	    words_a = set(a.lower().split())
910	    words_b = set(b.lower().split())
911	    if not words_a or not words_b:
912	        return 0.0
913	    intersection = len(words_a & words_b)
914	    union = len(words_a | words_b)
915	    return intersection / union if union else 0.0
916

918	# ── Bibliography ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
919	
920	
921	def _build_bibliography(section_claims: dict[str, list[dict]]) -> list[dict]:
922	    """Build global bibliography from all claims, one entry per unique URL.
923	
924	    W3.9: Dedup by canonical URL (scheme/host/www/trailing-slash/tracking
925	    params normalized) so http vs https or www vs non-www variants of the
926	    same page don't produce duplicate bibliography entries.
927	    """
928	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.citation_mapper import _canonicalize_url
929	
930	    url_to_best: dict[str, dict] = {}
931	    url_to_evidence_ids: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
932	    # Keep one canonical → display-url mapping so we preserve the nicer URL
933	    # (usually the https / no-www one) in the final bibliography entry.
934	    canonical_to_display: dict[str, str] = {}
935	
936	    for claims in section_claims.values():
937	        for claim in claims:
938	            url = claim.get("source_url", "")
939	            if not url:
940	                continue
941	            canonical = _canonicalize_url(url) or url
942	            # Prefer the canonical form as the display url, falling back to
943	            # first-seen if canonicalization produced an empty string.
944	            if canonical not in canonical_to_display:
945	                canonical_to_display[canonical] = canonical or url
946	            # Track all evidence IDs for this source
947	            eid = claim.get("evidence_id", "")
948	            if canonical not in url_to_evidence_ids:
949	                url_to_evidence_ids[canonical] = []
950	            if eid and eid not in url_to_evidence_ids[canonical]:
951	                url_to_evidence_ids[canonical].append(eid)
952	            # Track best evidence for metadata
953	            if canonical not in url_to_best:
954	                url_to_best[canonical] = claim
955	            elif claim.get("relevance_score", 0) > url_to_best[canonical].get("relevance_score", 0):
956	                url_to_best[canonical] = claim
957	
958	    # FIX-DEDUP-PAPER: collapse entries that point to the same paper at
959	    # different URLs (publisher vs PMC mirror vs DOI). The first-pass URL
960	    # canonicalization handles www/http/trailing-slash variants but cannot
961	    # detect that `thelancet.com/.../PIIS2589-5370(24)00098-1` and
962	    # `pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10945168` are the same article. We
963	    # merge by DOI and by PMID (extracted from PMC URLs) when available.
964	    def _extract_pmid(u: str) -> str:
965	        m = re.search(r"/articles/PMC(\d+)", u or "")
966	        return f"pmc{m.group(1)}" if m else ""
967	
968	    def _extract_regulatory_id(u: str) -> str:
969	        """PATCH-C: Collapse FDA/EMA labels across revisions.
970	
971	        FDA accessdata.fda.gov path structure:
972	          /drugsatfda_docs/label/<YEAR>/<NDC>s<REV>lbl.pdf
973	        The <NDC> (application number) identifies the drug, not the
974	        revision. Four WEGOVY labels 215256s000/s007/s024/s033 all share
975	        NDC=215256 and should collapse to one bibliography entry, as
976	        observed in PG_LB_SA_01 which cited [26][27][28][29] for four
977	        revisions of the same document.
978	
979	        EMA ema.europa.eu path structure:
980	          /en/documents/product-information/<product>-epar-product-information_en.pdf
981	        The <product> slug identifies the drug across revisions.
982	        """
983	        # FDA: capture application number before 's<rev>'
984	        m = re.search(
985	            r"/drugsatfda_docs/label/\d+/(\d+)s\d+lbl\.pdf",
986	            u or "",
987	            re.IGNORECASE,
988	        )
989	        if m:
990	            return f"fda-{m.group(1)}"
991	        # EMA: capture product slug before '-epar-product-information'
992	        m = re.search(
993	            r"/product-information/([^/]+?)-epar-product-information",
994	            u or "",
995	            re.IGNORECASE,
996	        )
997	        if m:
998	            return f"ema-{m.group(1).lower()}"
999	        return ""

1001	    def _extract_doi(best_claim: dict, url: str) -> str:
1002	        doi = (best_claim.get("doi", "") or "").strip().lower()
1003	        if doi:
1004	            return doi
1005	        # Also accept DOI embedded in URL
1006	        m = re.search(r"10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s\?#]+", url or "")
1007	        return (m.group(0).lower() if m else "")

1009	    # PATCH-D: OpenAlex work_id lookup for academic sources. Gives us

1024	                openalex_map[canonical] = w

1027	
1028	    paper_key_to_canonical: dict[str, str] = {}
1029	    canonical_to_merge_targets: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
1030	    for canonical, best in url_to_best.items():

1052

1070	        authors = best.get("authors", [])

1226	def _slugify(title: str) -> str:
1227	    """Convert section title to filename slug."""
1228	    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", title.lower()).strip("_")
1229	    return slug[:60]

58	def _build_compose_system() -> str:

60	    base_rules = _load_base_rules()
61	    base_block = f"\nBASE RULES FROM PROJECT:\n{base_rules}\n" if base_rules else ""

64	    # analytical angles in each section, targeting depth + completeness

66	    if WIKI_5LENS_ENABLED:

69	ANALYTICAL SCAFFOLD — Cover 5 angles in each section (integrated prose, NOT explicit headers):
70	
71	LENS 1 — EVIDENCE: Key quantified findings. EVERY numeric finding MUST include the effect size AND its confidence interval (or sample size and p-value) when the source provides them. Do not state a number without its statistical context. Example: "removal efficiency reached 92% (95% CI 87-95%, n=342) [REF:3]" — not "removal efficiency reached 92% [REF:3]". Every number gets [REF:N].
72	
73	LENS 2 — MECHANISM: How and why the observed effects occur. Causal chains. Physiological/biological pathways. Cite supporting mechanistic studies with [REF:N].
74	
75	LENS 3 — COMPARISON: Contrast findings across studies, populations, protocols, or interventions. Highlight where evidence converges vs diverges. Use comparative language.
76	
77	LENS 4 — CRITIQUE: Contradictions, limitations, caveats, methodological concerns. Which studies disagree? What populations are underrepresented? What are the boundaries of applicability?
78	
79	LENS 5 — HORIZON: Gaps in current knowledge. What questions remain unanswered? What would future research need to establish?

91	    # risk-of-bias assessment, GRADE per outcome). The pipeline does not
92	    # produce those artifacts. Calling the output a "systematic review" is a

95	{base_block}{five_lens_block}
96	ABSOLUTE RULES:
97	1. Write ONLY from the CLAIMS provided below. Do NOT add facts, statistics, or findings not in the claims.
98	2. Every factual statement MUST include its [REF:N] citation from the claims.
99	3. FIX-6 — INTERPRETIVE OVERLAY BAN. Uncited sentences are allowed ONLY as
100	   neutral connectives between two [REF:N]-cited sentences. Sentences that
101	   introduce NEW ideas — "the most robust glycemic signal is...", "clinical
102	   translation should...", "clinicians should therefore...", "this framing
103	   provides public-health context...", "adherence is the first-order
104	   mechanism...", "taken together, the evidence supports an X framing rather
105	   than a Y framing..." — are FORBIDDEN without a [REF:N]. If you cannot
106	   cite a claim, do NOT write it. No uncited clinical recommendations, no
107	   uncited "most robust / most important / best-studied" qualifiers, no
108	   uncited taxonomies, no uncited causal inferences, no uncited
109	   editorializing about research gaps. "Taken together" is a banned opener

112	   restate a source's title or scope (e.g. "A systematic review of X [REF:3]
113	   establishes that X has been evaluated"). Every sentence must report a
114	   specific finding, number, or causal link — not merely that a source
115	   exists. Thin atomic facts should be integrated into comparative
116	   paragraphs or dropped entirely, not written as solo sentences.

118	   multiple pooled outcomes, you must either (a) report all primary
119	   outcomes the source analyzed or (b) note explicitly that you are
120	   reporting one outcome among several. Do NOT present a single
121	   statistically significant finding from a predominantly-null study as if
122	   it were the study's headline result.
123	
124	FIX-HALLUC-1 — ABSOLUTE HALLUCINATION BAN (wiki composer):
125	Use ONLY the CLAIMS provided. Do NOT pull from training knowledge, do NOT recall studies,
126	papers, PMIDs, author names, sample sizes, regulatory bodies, or quoted expert reactions
127	unless the specific fact is present in one of the CLAIMS and tagged with a [REF:N] marker.
128	Under NO circumstance may the prose invent or recall: PMIDs, DOIs, author names (e.g.,
129	"Zhong et al.", "Manoogian"), sample sizes (e.g., "20,078 adults"), follow-up durations,
130	percent risk ratios (e.g., "91%"), study locations, cohort names, regulatory agency positions
131	(FDA/EFSA/EMA/FTC/Health Canada), media outlets (e.g., "Science Media Centre"), or expert
132	critiques — unless the specific fact comes from a CLAIM with a [REF:N] marker. If the prose
133	needs such a fact and no claim supports it, write a neutral qualitative sentence without
134	specifics (e.g., "observational data have raised long-term safety questions") rather than
135	fabricating a number or proper noun. Sections containing uncited specific numbers or
136	uncited named entities WILL BE REJECTED by the post-synthesis audit.
137

139	unless subsequent sections in the claim set actually cover them. Do not introduce axes,
140	frameworks, or specific studies that are not anchored in the provided CLAIMS.
141	4. Write in third person, academic register. No first person.
142	5. Include a **Key Findings** section at the end with 3-5 bullet points summarizing the most important claims, each with [REF:N] citations.

145	8. Do NOT include chain-of-thought, planning, reasoning, or meta-commentary about the writing process.

192	def _format_claims_for_prompt(claims: list[dict]) -> str:
193	    """Format claims as numbered input for the LLM."""
194	    lines = []
195	    dropped_no_ref = 0
196	    dropped_no_statement = 0
197	    for claim in claims:
198	        ref = claim.get("ref_num", 0)
199	        statement = claim.get("statement", "")
200	        quote = claim.get("direct_quote", "")
201	
202	        if not statement:
203	            dropped_no_statement += 1
204	            continue
205	        if not ref:
206	            # Was previously silent. Loud now so upstream url_to_ref
207	            # mismatches surface as a warning rather than a zero-citation
208	            # section with hallucinated prose.
209	            dropped_no_ref += 1
210	            continue
211	        lines.append(f"CLAIM [REF:{ref}]: {statement}")
212	        if quote and quote.lower() != statement.lower():
213	            lines.append(f"  QUOTE: \"{quote[:200]}\"")
214	
215	    if dropped_no_ref or dropped_no_statement:
216	        logger.warning(
217	            "[wiki-compose] _format_claims_for_prompt dropped %d claims: "
218	            "%d without ref_num, %d without statement (of %d total)",
219	            dropped_no_ref + dropped_no_statement,
220	            dropped_no_ref, dropped_no_statement, len(claims),
221	        )
222	
223	    return "\n".join(lines)
224

226	async def _compose_one_section(
227	    client: OpenRouterClient,
228	    section_title: str,
229	    section_description: str,
230	    claims: list[dict],
231	    section_order: int,
232	    total_sections: int,
233	    prev_summaries: str,
234	    query: str,
235	    unsupported_spans: list[str] | None = None,
236	) -> str:
237	    """Compose one report section from pre-cited claims."""
238	    # Sort by relevance (best evidence first)
239	    sorted_claims = sorted(
240	        claims, key=lambda c: c.get("relevance_score", 0), reverse=True,
241	    )
242	
243	    # Cap claims for context window
244	    if len(sorted_claims) > MAX_CLAIMS_PER_SECTION:
245	        sorted_claims = sorted_claims[:MAX_CLAIMS_PER_SECTION]
246	
247	    claims_text = _format_claims_for_prompt(sorted_claims)
248	
249	    # FIX-DIVERSITY: compute the source-diversity floor for THIS section.
250	    # G-Eval found sections under-citing their available source pool
251	    # (e.g., 4 of 14 = 29%). Composer prompt now reports the concrete floor.
252	    unique_refs_available = len({c.get("ref_num", 0) for c in sorted_claims if c.get("ref_num")})
253	    diversity_floor = max(1, int(unique_refs_available * 0.6))
254	
255	    # Determine target words
256	    if len(sorted_claims) < THIN_SECTION_THRESHOLD:
257	        target_words = "300-500"
258	        thin_note = (
259	            "\nNOTE: Limited evidence available for this section. Write a concise "
260	            "section focusing on what IS known. Acknowledge evidence gaps explicitly."
261	        )
262	    else:
263	        target_words = str(TARGET_WORDS_DEFAULT)
264	        thin_note = ""
265	
266	    # Load domain fragment
267	    domain_fragment = _load_prompt_fragment(section_title)
268	    if domain_fragment:
269	        domain_fragment = f"\nDOMAIN GUIDANCE:\n{domain_fragment}\n"
270	
271	    # Build previous sections context
272	    prev_block = ""
273	    transition_directive = ""
274	    if prev_summaries:
275	        prev_block = f"\nPREVIOUS SECTIONS (do NOT repeat their content):\n{prev_summaries}\n"
276	        # FIX-COHERENCE: Force an explicit bridge from the prior section.
277	        # G-Eval coherence dropped to 7/10 because adjacent sections jumped
278	        # topics without connecting language. The prompt now requires a
279	        # transition sentence in the FIRST paragraph that names the prior
280	        # section and explains the conceptual link to this one.
281	        if section_order > 1:
282	            transition_directive = (
283	                f"\nTRANSITION REQUIREMENT: The first paragraph MUST open with one\n"
284	                f"sentence that bridges from the prior section to this section.\n"
285	                f"Name what was just covered, then explain why this section follows\n"
286	                f"as a logical next step. Vary your bridging language across sections —\n"
287	                f"do not start every section with the same phrase. Examples of\n"
288	                f"acceptable openers (use a different one each time): \"Whereas the\n"
289	                f"prior section established X, this section turns to Y...\", \"With X\n"
290	                f"now characterized, the next question is Y...\", \"Given the X just\n"
291	                f"described, we now address Y...\", \"The X reviewed above raises a\n"
292	                f"second question: Y...\". This transition sentence does NOT need a\n"
293	                f"citation.\n"
294	            )
295	
296	    # FIX-HALLUC-REMEDIATE: When rewriting a flagged section, inject the
297	    # specific unsupported spans so the LLM knows what to avoid.
298	    remediation_block = ""
299	    if unsupported_spans:
300	        span_list = "\n".join(f"  - {s}" for s in unsupported_spans[:10])
301	        remediation_block = (
302	            f"\nREMEDIATION — REWRITE MODE: A prior draft of this section was audited "
303	            f"by an NLI hallucination detector and the following sentences were flagged "
304	            f"as UNSUPPORTED by the provided claims:\n{span_list}\n\n"
305	            f"Do NOT include these sentences or similar unsupported claims in your "
306	            f"rewrite. Write ONLY from the CLAIMS below. If a topic cannot be "
307	            f"substantiated by a claim, omit it entirely rather than stating it "
308	            f"without a [REF:N] citation.\n"
309	        )
310	
311	    prompt = (
312	        f"Write section {section_order}/{total_sections}: \"{section_title}\"\n"
313	        # FIX-NOT-SYSTEMATIC: "evidence review" replaces "systematic review".
314	        f"of an evidence review answering: \"{query}\"\n"
315	        f"\nSection focus: {section_description}\n"
316	        f"{thin_note}"
317	        f"{domain_fragment}"
318	        f"{remediation_block}"
319	        f"{prev_block}"
320	        f"{transition_directive}"
321	        f"\nCLAIMS (use ONLY these, cite with [REF:N]):\n"
322	        f"{claims_text}\n"
323	        f"\nSOURCE DIVERSITY FLOOR: this section has {unique_refs_available} unique sources\n"
324	        f"in the claims. Your composed prose MUST cite at least {diversity_floor} of\n"
325	        f"them (60% floor). Recycling fewer is a quality defect.\n"
326	        f"\nWrite the section now. Target {target_words} words."
327	    )
328	
329	    result = await client.generate(
330	        prompt=prompt,
331	        system=COMPOSE_SYSTEM,
332	        max_tokens=COMPOSE_MAX_TOKENS,
333	        temperature=0.3,
334	        timeout=COMPOSE_TIMEOUT,
335	        reasoning_exclude=True,
336	    )
337	
338	    return result.content
339

344	async def compose_from_wiki(
345	    client: OpenRouterClient,
346	    wiki_result: WikiResult,
347	    query: str,
348	    outline: list[dict],
349	) -> dict:
350	    """
351	    Compose a full report from wiki pages.
352	
353	    Returns a dict matching the exact contract of synthesize_report():
354	    final_report, sections, bibliography, quality_metrics, etc.
355	    """
356	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.section_writer import _scrub_cot
357	
358	    sections = []
359	    prev_summaries = ""
360	    section_evidence_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
361	    failed_sections: list[str] = []
362	
363	    total_sections = len(outline)
364	
365	    for i, sec in enumerate(outline):
366	        sid = sec.get("section_id", f"s{i:02d}")
367	        title = sec.get("title", "Unknown")
368	        description = sec.get("description", title)
369	        claims = wiki_result.section_claims.get(sid, [])
370	
371	        if not claims:
372	            logger.warning("[wiki-compose] Section %s '%s' has 0 claims — skipping", sid, title[:40])
373	            failed_sections.append(sid)
374	            continue
375	
376	        logger.info(
377	            "[wiki-compose] Composing %s: '%s' (%d claims, %d sources)",
378	            sid, title[:50], len(claims),
379	            len({c.get("source_url") for c in claims}),
380	        )
381	
382	        # Track which evidence was provided
383	        section_evidence_map[sid] = [
384	            c.get("evidence_id", "") for c in claims if c.get("evidence_id")
385	        ]
386	
387	        # Compose with retry. Both exceptions AND empty content trigger retry —
388	        # reasoning models (gpt-5/o3) sometimes return HTTP 200 with empty content
389	        # when the reasoning budget consumes the full token allowance.
390	        content = ""
391	        for attempt in range(2):
392	            try:
393	                content = await _compose_one_section(
394	                    client=client,
395	                    section_title=title,
396	                    section_description=description,
397	                    claims=claims,
398	                    section_order=i + 1,
399	                    total_sections=total_sections,
400	                    prev_summaries=prev_summaries,
401	                    query=query,
402	                )
403	                if content and content.strip():
404	                    break
405	                # Empty content — log and retry (or fail on second attempt)
406	                if attempt == 0:
407	                    logger.warning(
408	                        "[wiki-compose] Section %s attempt 1 returned empty content "
409	                        "(possible reasoning budget overflow) — retrying",
410	                        sid,
411	                    )
412	                else:
413	                    logger.error(
414	                        "[wiki-compose] Section %s returned empty content twice — skipping",
415	                        sid,
416	                    )
417	                    failed_sections.append(sid)
418	            except Exception as exc:
419	                if attempt == 0:
420	                    logger.warning(
421	                        "[wiki-compose] Section %s attempt 1 failed: %s — retrying",
422	                        sid, str(exc)[:100],
423	                    )
424	                else:
425	                    logger.error(
426	                        "[wiki-compose] Section %s failed after 2 attempts: %s — skipping",
427	                        sid, str(exc)[:100],
428	                    )
429	                    failed_sections.append(sid)
430	
431	        if not content or not content.strip():
432	            continue
433	
434	        # Scrub CoT leakage
435	        content = _scrub_cot(content)
436	
437	        # Deterministic citation resolution.
438	        # Models intermittently emit [REF:N], [CITE:N], or [Ref:N] despite the
439	        # system prompt — collapse all numeric variants to canonical [N].
440	        # Validated against gpt-4o-mini output (Section 3 leaked [CITE:N]).
441	        content = re.sub(r"\[(?:REF|CITE|Ref|Cite|ref|cite):(\d+)\]", r"[\1]", content)
442	
443	        # Remove duplicate section headers the LLM may have generated
444	        # (the assembler adds ## Title, so strip any leading # Title from content)
445	        content = re.sub(
446	            r"^\s*#{1,3}\s+" + re.escape(title[:30]) + r"[^\n]*\n+",
447	            "",
448	            content,
449	            count=1,
450	            flags=re.IGNORECASE,
451	        )
452	        content = content.lstrip()
453	
454	        # Count metrics
455	        word_count = len(content.split())
456	        citation_nums = re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", content)
457	        unique_citations = sorted(set(int(n) for n in citation_nums))
458	
459	        sections.append({
460	            "section_id": sid,
461	            "title": title,
462	            "content": content,
463	            "word_count": word_count,
464	            "citation_ids": [f"[{n}]" for n in unique_citations],
465	            "evidence_ids": section_evidence_map.get(sid, []),
466	        })
467	
468	        logger.info("  → %d words, %d citations", word_count, len(unique_citations))
469	
470	        # Build summary for next section context (text + cited REFs)
471	        sentences = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+", content)
472	        summary = " ".join(sentences[:2]) if sentences else ""
473	        cited_refs = ", ".join(f"[{n}]" for n in unique_citations[:10])
474	        prev_summaries += f"- {title}: {summary[:200]} (cited: {cited_refs})\n"
475	
476	    # ── Generate abstract ────────────────────────────────────────
477	    abstract = await _compose_abstract(client, query, sections, wiki_result.bibliography)
478	
479	    # ── FIX-PRISMA-METHODS: Methods section ─────────────────────
480	    total_evidence = sum(
481	        len(claims) for claims in wiki_result.section_claims.values()
482	    )
483	    # search_rounds and persona_count aren't directly available on WikiResult,
484	    # so we pass conservative defaults; the Methods prose handles unknown cases.
485	    methods_section = _build_methods_section(
486	        query=query,
487	        bibliography=wiki_result.bibliography,
488	        evidence_count=total_evidence,
489	        iteration_count=1,
490	        search_rounds=int(os.getenv("PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS", "2")),
491	        persona_count=5,
492	    )
493	
494	    # ── Assemble final report ────────────────────────────────────
495	    final_report = _assemble_report(
496	        query, abstract, sections, wiki_result.bibliography,
497	        methods=methods_section,
498	    )
499	
500	    # ── Partial failure check ────────────────────────────────────
501	    composed_count = len(sections)
502	    if composed_count < total_sections * 0.5 and total_sections > 0:
503	        logger.error(
504	            "[wiki-compose] PARTIAL FAILURE: only %d/%d sections composed",
505	            composed_count, total_sections,
506	        )
507	        status = "partial_failure"
508	    else:
509	        status = "complete"
510	
511	    # ── Quality metrics (reuse existing compute_quality_metrics) ──
512	    total_words = sum(s["word_count"] for s in sections)
513	    total_citations = sum(len(s["citation_ids"]) for s in sections)
514	    unique_sources = len(wiki_result.bibliography)
515	    zero_cite_sections = sum(1 for s in sections if not s["citation_ids"])
516	
517	    # Build quality metrics inline (compute_quality_metrics expects TypedDict
518	    # EvidencePiece/ReportSection which our wiki dicts don't match exactly)
519	    all_evidence = wiki_result.unassigned_evidence + [
520	        c for cl in wiki_result.section_claims.values() for c in cl
521	    ]
522	    gold_count = sum(1 for e in all_evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "GOLD")
523	    silver_count = sum(1 for e in all_evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "SILVER")
524	
525	    # W3.11: Use the canonical faithfulness helper so state.faithfulness_score
526	    # and quality_metrics.faithfulness_score stay in sync. Computed from the
527	    # actual claims assigned into wiki sections — same definition both paths
528	    # use. The earlier -1.0 sentinel was a "G-Eval does it externally" stub
529	    # but in practice callers treated it as a bug (dual source of truth).
530	    from src.polaris_graph.agents.synthesizer import compute_faithfulness
531	    _wiki_claims = [
532	        c for cl in wiki_result.section_claims.values() for c in cl
533	    ]
534	    _wiki_faith = compute_faithfulness(_wiki_claims)
535	
536	    quality = {
537	        "total_words": total_words,
538	        "total_sections": len(sections),
539	        "total_citations": total_citations,
540	        "unique_sources": unique_sources,
541	        "total_evidence": len(all_evidence),
542	        "gold_evidence": gold_count,
543	        "silver_evidence": silver_count,
544	        "bronze_evidence": len(all_evidence) - gold_count - silver_count,
545	        "faithfulness_score": _wiki_faith,
546	        "avg_citations_per_section": (
547	            total_citations / max(len(sections), 1)
548	        ),
549	    }
550	
551	    # Augment with wiki-specific metrics
552	    quality["zero_cite_sections"] = zero_cite_sections
553	    quality["failed_sections"] = len(failed_sections)
554	    quality["wiki_claims"] = wiki_result.stats.get("total_claims_in_wiki", 0)
555	
556	    # Quality gate check (log, don't block)
557	    gate_failures = []
558	    if total_words < 2000:
559	        gate_failures.append(f"words={total_words}<2000")
560	    if total_citations < 5:
561	        gate_failures.append(f"citations={total_citations}<5")
562	    if unique_sources < 5:
563	        gate_failures.append(f"sources={unique_sources}<5")
564	    if zero_cite_sections > 0:
565	        gate_failures.append(f"zero_cite_sections={zero_cite_sections}")
566	
567	    gate_result = "passed" if not gate_failures else f"failed: {', '.join(gate_failures)}"
568	    if gate_failures:
569	        logger.warning("[wiki-compose] Quality gate: %s", gate_result)
570	    else:
571	        logger.info("[wiki-compose] Quality gate: PASSED")
572	
573	    # ── Build evidence chain (RAGAS compatibility — full fields) ──
574	    evidence_chain = []
575	    for claims in wiki_result.section_claims.values():
576	        for claim in claims:
577	            evidence_chain.append({
578	                "evidence_id": claim.get("evidence_id", ""),
579	                "source_url": claim.get("source_url", ""),
580	                "source_title": claim.get("source_title", ""),
581	                "source_type": claim.get("source_type", ""),
582	                "statement": claim.get("statement", ""),
583	                "direct_quote": claim.get("direct_quote", ""),
584	                "quality_tier": claim.get("quality_tier", ""),
585	                "relevance_score": claim.get("relevance_score", 0.0),
586	                "perspective": claim.get("perspective", ""),
587	                "methodology": claim.get("methodology", ""),
588	                "conditions": claim.get("conditions", ""),
589	                "limitations": claim.get("limitations", ""),
590	                "strength_signals": claim.get("strength_signals", []),
591	                "year": claim.get("year"),
592	                "authors": claim.get("authors", []),
593	                "doi": claim.get("doi"),
594	            })
595	
596	    # ── Build section_outline for output ─────────────────────────
597	    section_outline = [
598	        {
599	            "section_id": s.get("section_id", ""),
600	            "title": s.get("title", ""),
601	            "description": s.get("description", ""),
602	            "evidence_ids": s.get("evidence_ids", []),
603	            "target_words": s.get("target_words", TARGET_WORDS_DEFAULT),
604	            "order": s.get("order", i),
605	        }
606	        for i, s in enumerate(outline)
607	    ]
608	
609	    logger.info(
610	        "[wiki-compose] Report complete: %d words, %d citations, %d sources, gate=%s",
611	        total_words, total_citations, unique_sources, gate_result,
612	    )
613	
614	    # HONEST-REBUILD Phase 1b (plan: C:/Users/msn/.claude/plans/lovely-finding-firefly.md)
615	    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
616	    # The post-synthesis hallucination audit, the REMEDIATE-LOOP section
617	    # rewrite mechanism, the FIX-3 abstract re-audit, and the PATCH-B
618	    # cross-section POLISH pass were all removed here per the 2026-04-17
619	    # PG_LB_SA_02 content audit findings:
620	    #
621	    #   - REMEDIATE-LOOP was documented NLI-gaming: iter-2 compressed
622	    #     sections 60%% on average by deleting synthesis content to beat
623	    #     the NLI metric, at the cost of evidence interpretation (Section B
624	    #     of PG_LB_SA_02_CONTENT_AUDIT.md).
625	    #   - The hallucination detector NLI check uses same-family self-grading
626	    #     (MiniCheck flan-t5-large against prose generated by the same
627	    #     family Kimi/Qwen/GLM). Evaluator-generator family collapse per
628	    #     Play Favorites (arXiv 2508.06709) and DeepHalluBench (arXiv
629	    #     2601.22984, Jan 2026).
630	    #   - PATCH-B POLISH crashed in PG_LB_SA_02 with a kwarg mismatch
631	    #     (LoopbackLLMClient.generate got unexpected keyword argument
632	    #     'call_type'); see BUG-POLISH-CALLTYPE in logs/bug_log.md.
633	    #     The crash was the visible symptom; the structural issue is
634	    #     that POLISH is itself same-family self-grading.
635	    #
636	    # Replacement: Phase 5 of the honest-rebuild plan wires a non-same-family
637	    # external evaluator (DeepSeek V3.2 generator + Qwen 3 32B evaluator,
638	    # verified family-distance per loopback/audit/_open_source_models_2026.md)
639	    # as the single external quality signal. No self-grading, no survivorship-
640	    # biased re-scoring, no rewrite-to-pass-metric loop.
641	    hallucination_audit: list[dict] = []  # empty for backwards compat with callers
642	    # final_report was already assembled at line ~495 with (pre-rewrite)
643	    # sections; that is the honest output. No re-assembly needed here.
644	
645	
646	    # FIX-2: Defense-in-depth — final sweep on the assembled report.
647	    # If any [REF:N] / [CITE:N] token leaked through (e.g. from a non-remediated
648	    # section writer or a code path that wasn't covered), normalize here and log
649	    # a loud warning so the leak is diagnosed rather than silently shipped.
650	    _leak_pattern = re.compile(r"\[(?:REF|CITE|Ref|Cite|ref|cite):(\d+)\]")
651	    _leaks = _leak_pattern.findall(final_report)
652	    if _leaks:
653	        logger.warning(
654	            "[wiki-compose] FIX-2 leak guard: %d [REF:N]/[CITE:N] tokens survived to final_report — normalizing. Sample: %s",
655	            len(_leaks), _leaks[:5],
656	        )
657	        final_report = _leak_pattern.sub(r"[\1]", final_report)
658	        # Also normalize per-section content so downstream consumers of the
659	        # `sections` list (e.g. hallucination_audit display) see the same text.
660	        for _s in sections:
661	            if "content" in _s:
662	                _s["content"] = _leak_pattern.sub(r"[\1]", _s["content"])
663	
664	    return {
665	        "section_outline": section_outline,
666	        "sections": sections,
667	        "bibliography": wiki_result.bibliography,
668	        "evidence_chain": evidence_chain,
669	        "draft_report": final_report,
670	        "final_report": final_report,
671	        "evidence_clusters": [],
672	        "quality_metrics": quality,
673	        "status": status,
674	        "converged": status == "complete" and not gate_failures,
675	        "convergence_reason": f"wiki_synthesis: {gate_result}",
676	        "quality_gate_result": gate_result,
677	        "section_evidence_map": section_evidence_map,
678	        "expansion_passes_used": 0,
679	        "hallucination_audit": hallucination_audit,
680	        "gap_queries": [],
681	    }
