687	async def _compose_abstract(
688	    client: OpenRouterClient,
689	    query: str,
690	    sections: list[dict],
691	    bibliography: list[dict],
692	    unsupported_spans: list[str] | None = None,
693	) -> str:
694	    """Generate a grounded abstract from section summaries.
695	
696	    FIX-3: If `unsupported_spans` is provided, include them in the prompt so
697	    the rewrite is targeted — same pattern as section-level remediation.
698	    """
699	    summaries = "\n".join(
700	        f"- {s['title']}: {s['content'][:300]}..."
701	        for s in sections
702	    )
703	
704	    unsupported_block = ""
705	    if unsupported_spans:
706	        spans_text = "\n".join(f"  - {s[:150]}" for s in unsupported_spans[:5])
707	        unsupported_block = (
708	            "\n\nCRITICAL — The previous abstract draft contained these UNSUPPORTED\n"
709	            "spans (flagged by NLI audit against the evidence pool):\n"
710	            f"{spans_text}\n"
711	            "Do NOT reproduce these claims. Every sentence in the new abstract must\n"
712	            "paraphrase content that appears in the section summaries above.\n"
713	        )
714	
715	    prompt = (
716	        # FIX-NOT-SYSTEMATIC: "evidence review" rather than "systematic review".
717	        f"Write a 200-word abstract for an evidence review answering: \"{query}\"\n\n"
718	        f"Section summaries:\n{summaries}\n\n"
719	        f"RULES:\n"
720	        f"- Include 2-3 key quantitative findings, each followed by a citation\n"
721	        f"  in the form [3] where 3 is a real number from 1 to {len(bibliography)}\n"
722	        f"- NEVER write the literal token [N] — this is a placeholder, not a citation\n"
723	        f"- State the scope (number of sources, topics covered)\n"
724	        f"- Note major limitations or evidence gaps\n"
725	        f"- Academic register, third person\n"

760	# ── Cross-Section Polish (PATCH-B, STORM pattern) ────────────────────
761	
762	
763	async def _polish_cross_section(
764	    client: OpenRouterClient,
765	    query: str,
766	    sections: list[dict],
767	) -> list[dict]:
768	    """PATCH-B: STORM-style cross-section polish pass.
769	
770	    Source: stanford-oval/storm/knowledge_storm/storm_wiki/modules/
771	    article_polish.py PolishPageModule, called with remove_duplicate=True.
772	    STORM's prompt instructs the LLM to act as "a faithful text editor
773	    that is good at finding repeated information in the article and
774	    deleting them" while preserving "inline citations and article
775	    structure (indicated by '#', '##', etc.)".
776	
777	    We adapt it to also catch the cross-section contradiction class
778	    found in PG_LB_SA_01 §Risks line 91, where a section disclaimed
779	    six safety signals as "not characterized here" while other sections
780	    of the same document characterized each of them.
781	
782	    Two defect classes are repaired in one pass:
783	
784	    1. DISCLAIMERS-THAT-CONTRADICT-CONTENT: a sentence that says a
785	       topic is "not characterized here" / "not substantiated by the
786	       claims" — when another section DOES characterize that topic.
787	       Repair: delete the false disclaimer.
788	
789	    2. DUPLICATE CLAIMS: the same numeric finding restated across
790	       sections with the same citation.  Repair: keep the most
791	       contextual occurrence.
792	
793	    HARD RULES (enforced via prompt):
794	    - Every [N] citation preserved.
795	    - Every '## Title' heading preserved.
796	    - No new facts added. Only removal / shortening.
797	
798	    Returns the polished sections with `content` replaced per-section.
799	    If the LLM output is suspicious (too short, missing headings) the
800	    original sections are returned unchanged.
801	    """
802	    if len(sections) < 2:
803	        return sections
804	
805	    # Concatenate sections with their H2 headings so the LLM sees the
806	    # full document context.
807	    section_texts = []
808	    for s in sections:
809	        title = s.get("title", "")
810	        content = s.get("content", "")
811	        section_texts.append(f"## {title}\n\n{content}")
812	    joined = "\n\n".join(section_texts)
813	
814	    system = (
815	        "You are a faithful text editor. You find two kinds of defects "
816	        "in multi-section evidence reports and repair them:\n\n"
817	        "(A) DISCLAIMERS-THAT-CONTRADICT-CONTENT. A sentence that says a "
818	        "topic is 'not characterized here' / 'not substantiated by the "
819	        "claims' / 'not covered in this section' — when another section "
820	        "of the same document DOES characterize that topic. Repair: "
821	        "delete the false disclaimer sentence. Do not invent new content.\n\n"
822	        "(B) DUPLICATE CLAIMS. The same numeric finding stated twice "
823	        "across different sections with the same citation. Repair: keep "
824	        "the most contextual occurrence, delete the other. Do not merge "
825	        "citations into unrelated sentences.\n\n"
826	        "HARD RULES:\n"
827	        "- Preserve every [N] citation marker exactly. Do not renumber.\n"
828	        "- Preserve every '## Title' heading. Do not rename sections.\n"
829	        "- Do not add new factual claims. Only remove or shorten.\n"
830	        "- Return the full edited report, section headings intact.\n"
831	    )
832	    prompt = (
833	        f"Original research question: {query}\n\n"
834	        f"Report to polish:\n\n{joined}\n\n"
835	        "Return the polished report. Preserve all ## headings and "
836	        "[N] citations. If no defects are found, return the report "
837	        "unchanged."
838	    )
839	
840	    try:
841	        import asyncio as _aio
842	        polished = await _aio.wait_for(
843	            client.generate(
844	                prompt=prompt,
845	                system=system,
846	                max_tokens=COMPOSE_MAX_TOKENS,
847	                temperature=0.1,
848	                call_type="generate:polish",
849	            ),
850	            timeout=COMPOSE_TIMEOUT,
851	        )
852	    except Exception as exc:
853	        logger.warning(
854	            "[wiki-compose] POLISH: cross-section polish LLM call failed: "
855	            "%s — shipping unpolished",
856	            str(exc)[:200],
857	        )
858	        return sections
859	
860	    if not polished or len(polished) < len(joined) * 0.5:
861	        logger.warning(
862	            "[wiki-compose] POLISH: polish output truncated (%d chars "
863	            "vs original %d) — shipping unpolished",
864	            len(polished or ""), len(joined),
865	        )
866	        return sections
867	
868	    # Re-split polished output by '## ' headings. Preserve original
869	    # section_ids and evidence_ids; only content text is replaced.
870	    polished = _scrub_cot(polished)
871	    polished = re.sub(
872	        r"\[(?:REF|CITE|Ref|Cite|ref|cite):(\d+)\]",
873	        r"[\1]",
874	        polished,
875	    )
876	    new_section_map: dict[str, str] = {}
877	    for block in re.split(r"(?m)^##\s+", polished):
878	        block = block.strip()
879	        if not block:
880	            continue
881	        first_nl = block.find("\n")
882	        if first_nl == -1:
883	            continue
884	        title = block[:first_nl].strip()
885	        content = block[first_nl + 1:].strip()
886	        new_section_map[title] = content
887	
888	    # Preserve [N] citation set — if the polish pass dropped any citations
889	    # entirely, reject and keep the original.
890	    original_citations = set(re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", joined))
891	    polished_citations = set(re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", polished))
892	    if not original_citations.issubset(polished_citations):
893	        dropped = original_citations - polished_citations
894	        logger.warning(
895	            "[wiki-compose] POLISH: dropped %d citations (%s) — rejecting "
896	            "polish, shipping unpolished",
897	            len(dropped), sorted(dropped)[:5],
898	        )
899	        return sections
900	
901	    polished_sections = []
902	    content_changes = 0
903	    for s in sections:
904	        title = s.get("title", "")
905	        new_content = new_section_map.get(title)
906	        if new_content is None:
907	            polished_sections.append(s)
908	            continue
909	        if new_content != s.get("content", ""):
910	            content_changes += 1
911	        polished_sec = dict(s)
912	        polished_sec["content"] = new_content
913	        polished_sections.append(polished_sec)
914	
915	    logger.info(
916	        "[wiki-compose] POLISH: cross-section polish applied "
917	        "(%d sections, %d changed, input=%d chars, output=%d chars)",
918	        len(polished_sections), content_changes, len(joined), len(polished),
919	    )
920	    return polished_sections

929	def _assemble_report(
930	    query: str,
931	    abstract: str,
932	    sections: list[dict],
933	    bibliography: list[dict],
934	    methods: str | None = None,
935	) -> str:
936	    """Assemble final markdown report from sections + bibliography.
937	
938	    FIX-PRISMA-METHODS: emits a Methods section describing the actual
939	    pipeline process (information sources, search phases, evidence
940	    extraction, filtering, verification) so readers can distinguish this
941	    agentic-research output from a PRISMA-conformant systematic review.
942	    """
943	    parts = [f"# {query}", ""]
944	
945	    if abstract:
946	        parts.extend(["## Abstract", "", abstract, ""])
947	
948	    if methods:
949	        parts.extend(["## Methods", "", methods, ""])
950	
951	    for section in sections:
952	        parts.extend([
953	            f"## {section['title']}",
954	            "",
955	            section["content"],
956	            "",
957	        ])
958	
959	    parts.extend(["## References", ""])
960	    for bib in bibliography:
961	        authors = ", ".join(bib.get("authors", [])[:3]) if bib.get("authors") else "Unknown"
962	        year = bib.get("year", "n.d.")
963	        title = bib.get("title", "Unknown")[:100]
964	        url = bib.get("url", "")
965	        doi = f" DOI: {bib['doi']}" if bib.get("doi") else ""
966	        parts.append(f"[{bib['ref_num']}] {authors} ({year}). {title}.{doi} {url}")
967	        parts.append("")
968	
969	    return "\n".join(parts)

972	def _build_methods_section(
973	    query: str,
974	    bibliography: list[dict],
975	    evidence_count: int,
976	    iteration_count: int,
977	    search_rounds: int,
978	    persona_count: int,
979	) -> str:
980	    """FIX-PRISMA-METHODS: a transparent Methods section that describes the
981	    agentic-research process honestly, rather than letting the prose of the
982	    review suggest PRISMA compliance it does not have.
983	    """
984	    n_sources = len(bibliography)
985	    peer_reviewed = sum(
986	        1 for b in bibliography
987	        if (b.get("source_type", "") or "").lower() in
988	        ("journal_article", "academic", "peer_reviewed")
989	    )
990	    non_primary = n_sources - peer_reviewed
991	
992	    # Classify source tiers by URL heuristics for honest disclosure.
993	    news_or_institutional_markers = (
994	        "news", "newsroom", "press", "today.", "blog", "sciencedaily",
995	        "medium.com", "facebook.com", "linkedin.com", "instagram.com",
996	        "twitter.com", "x.com", "reddit.com",
997	    )
998	    patient_ed_markers = (
999	        "hopkinsmedicine", "mayoclinic.org", "massgeneralbrigham",
1000	        "health.harvard.edu/blog", "healthline", "webmd", "clevelandclinic",
1001	    )
1002	    news_cnt = sum(
1003	        1 for b in bibliography
1004	        if any(m in (b.get("url", "") or "").lower() for m in news_or_institutional_markers)
1005	    )
1006	    patient_ed_cnt = sum(
1007	        1 for b in bibliography
1008	        if any(m in (b.get("url", "") or "").lower() for m in patient_ed_markers)
1009	    )
1010	
1011	    return (
1012	        "**This is an evidence review produced by an agentic research "
1013	        "pipeline (POLARIS/polaris_graph). It is NOT a PRISMA 2020 "
1014	        "systematic review: it has no protocol registration, no "
1015	        "pre-specified eligibility criteria, no dual-reviewer study "
1016	        "selection, no per-study risk-of-bias assessment, and no "
1017	        "outcome-level GRADE certainty grading. Readers should interpret "
1018	        "its claims accordingly.**\n\n"
1019	        "**Search and retrieval.** The pipeline generated multiple seed "
1020	        "search queries covering the STORM multi-perspective framework "
1021	        f"(Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public Health, "
1022	        "Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging Trends) and "
1023	        f"executed {search_rounds} round(s) of agentic web and academic "
1024	        "search via Serper, Semantic Scholar, and other configured "
1025	        "providers. Pages were fetched and summarized; sources that "
1026	        "returned Cloudflare challenges, cookie-consent stubs, or "
1027	        "navigation chrome were filtered.\n\n"
1028	        "**Evidence extraction.** Atomic facts were extracted from "
1029	        "retrieved source content, each with a direct verbatim quote from "
1030	        "the source, a structured category (statistic, methodology, "
1031	        "causal_link, risk, etc.), and a relevance score.\n\n"
1032	        f"**Evidence pool.** The synthesized evidence pool for this review "
1033	        f"contains {evidence_count} atomic fact(s) drawn from "
1034	        f"{n_sources} unique source(s): approximately {peer_reviewed} "
1035	        f"peer-reviewed journal article(s); "
1036	        f"{patient_ed_cnt} institutional patient-education or clinical-"
1037	        f"guidance page(s); {news_cnt} news-release, blog, or social "
1038	        f"post(s); and "
1039	        f"{max(0, non_primary - patient_ed_cnt - news_cnt)} other "
1040	        "source(s). Readers should weight evidence accordingly: claims "
1041	        "sourced to patient-education pages or news releases are NOT "
1042	        "equivalent to findings from peer-reviewed studies.\n\n"
1043	        "**Verification.** Each atomic fact was passed through NLI-based "
1044	        "verification (MiniCheck flan-t5-large) comparing the claim to a "
1045	        "quote-centered window of source text. Evidence with low "
1046	        "semantic support was down-tiered or filtered.\n\n"
1047	        "**Synthesis.** A STORM-derived outline with "
1048	        f"{persona_count} expert persona(s) guided the report structure. "
1049	        "Each section was composed from its assigned evidence pool, then "
1050	        "audited by an NLI hallucination detector; sections with "
1051	        "unsupported claims were rewritten using only the cited atomic "
1052	        "facts.\n\n"
1053	        "**Limitations of this format.** No PRISMA flow diagram, no "
1054	        "pre-specified PICO, no duplicate reviewer reconciliation, no "
1055	        "study-level risk-of-bias table, no outcome-level GRADE grid, "
1056	        "no sensitivity analyses, no publication-bias funnel plot. "
1057	        "Evidence filtering is embedding-cosine-similarity based and may "
1058	        "underweight evidence whose vocabulary diverges from the "
1059	        "query. Conclusions should be treated as indicative, not "
1060	        "conclusive."
1061	    )

1	"""
2	Wiki Deep Crawl — expands source pool via Jina Reader (free, 200 RPM).
3	
4	Levels:
5	  L1: Follow DOIs/PMIDs mentioned in fetched content
6	  L2: S2 citation graph — references for key papers
7	  L3: S2 recommendations for seed papers
8	
9	All fetching via Jina Reader ($0) with SQLite content cache dedup.
10	"""
11	
12	import asyncio
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import re
16	import time
17	from typing import Any
18	
19	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
20	
21	CRAWL_CONCURRENCY = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAWL_CONCURRENCY", "10"))
22	CRAWL_BUDGET_MINUTES = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAWL_BUDGET_MINUTES", "15"))
23	CRAWL_MAX_SOURCES = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAWL_MAX_SOURCES", "500"))
24	CRAWL_L1_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_CRAWL_L1_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
25	CRAWL_L2_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_CRAWL_L2_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
26	CRAWL_L2_MAX_REFS = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAWL_L2_MAX_REFS", "10"))
27	S2_API_KEY = os.getenv("SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY", "")
28	
29	# DOI/PMID extraction patterns
30	DOI_PATTERN = re.compile(r"10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s\])<>\"',;]+")
31	PMID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"(?:PMID|PubMed ID)[:\s]*(\d{7,8})")
32	PMC_PATTERN = re.compile(r"PMC\d{7,8}")
33	
34	
35	async def deep_crawl(
36	    fetched_content: list[dict],
37	    web_results: list[dict],
38	    academic_results: list[dict],
39	    query: str,
40	    vector_id: str,
41	) -> list[dict]:
42	    """
43	    Expand the source pool by following references and DOIs.
44	
45	    Returns additional fetched content (list of {url, content, title, source_type}).
46	    """
47	    start_time = time.time()
48	    deadline = start_time + CRAWL_BUDGET_MINUTES * 60
49	
50	    # Collect all already-fetched URLs for dedup
51	    known_urls = {fc.get("url", "") for fc in fetched_content}
52	    known_urls.update(r.get("url", "") for r in web_results)
53	    known_urls.update(r.get("url", "") for r in academic_results)
54	    known_urls.discard("")
55	
56	    logger.info(
57	        "[deep-crawl] Starting: %d known URLs, budget=%d min, max=%d sources",
58	        len(known_urls), CRAWL_BUDGET_MINUTES, CRAWL_MAX_SOURCES,
59	    )
60	
61	    new_content: list[dict] = []
62	
63	    # ── L1: Follow DOIs/PMIDs from fetched content ──────────────
64	    if CRAWL_L1_ENABLED and time.time() < deadline:
65	        l1_urls = _extract_dois_from_content(fetched_content, known_urls)
66	        if l1_urls:
67	            logger.info("[deep-crawl] L1: Found %d new DOI/PMID URLs to follow", len(l1_urls))
68	            l1_fetched = await _fetch_batch(
69	                l1_urls[:CRAWL_MAX_SOURCES // 3],
70	                known_urls, deadline,
71	            )
72	            new_content.extend(l1_fetched)
73	            known_urls.update(fc.get("url", "") for fc in l1_fetched)
74	            logger.info("[deep-crawl] L1: Fetched %d new sources", len(l1_fetched))
75	
76	    # ── L2: S2 citation graph ───────────────────────────────────
77	    if CRAWL_L2_ENABLED and time.time() < deadline:
78	        seed_papers = _get_seed_papers(academic_results)
79	        if seed_papers:
80	            l2_urls = await _get_s2_references(seed_papers, known_urls)
81	            if l2_urls:
82	                logger.info("[deep-crawl] L2: Found %d reference URLs to follow", len(l2_urls))
83	                l2_fetched = await _fetch_batch(
84	                    l2_urls[:CRAWL_MAX_SOURCES // 3],
85	                    known_urls, deadline,
86	                )
87	                new_content.extend(l2_fetched)
88	                known_urls.update(fc.get("url", "") for fc in l2_fetched)
89	                logger.info("[deep-crawl] L2: Fetched %d new sources", len(l2_fetched))
90	
91	    elapsed = time.time() - start_time
92	    logger.info(
93	        "[deep-crawl] Complete: %d new sources in %.1fs (L1+L2)",
94	        len(new_content), elapsed,
95	    )
96	
97	    return new_content

100	# ── L1: DOI/PMID Extraction ─────────────────────────────────────────
101	
102	
103	def _extract_dois_from_content(
104	    fetched_content: list[dict],
105	    known_urls: set[str],
106	) -> list[str]:
107	    """Extract DOIs and PMIDs from fetched content, return as URLs."""
108	    urls = []
109	    seen_dois: set[str] = set()
110	
111	    for fc in fetched_content:
112	        content = fc.get("content", "")
113	        if not content:
114	            continue
115	
116	        # Extract DOIs
117	        for doi_match in DOI_PATTERN.finditer(content):
118	            doi = doi_match.group(0).rstrip(".")
119	            if doi not in seen_dois:
120	                seen_dois.add(doi)
121	                url = f"https://doi.org/{doi}"
122	                if url not in known_urls:
123	                    urls.append(url)
124	
125	        # Extract PMIDs
126	        for pmid_match in PMID_PATTERN.finditer(content):
127	            pmid = pmid_match.group(1)
128	            url = f"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/{pmid}/"
129	            if url not in known_urls:
130	                urls.append(url)
131	
132	        # Extract PMC IDs
133	        for pmc_match in PMC_PATTERN.finditer(content):
134	            pmc_id = pmc_match.group(0)
135	            url = f"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/{pmc_id}/"
136	            if url not in known_urls:
137	                urls.append(url)
138	
139	    # Dedup
140	    seen = set()
141	    unique = []
142	    for url in urls:
143	        if url not in seen:
144	            seen.add(url)
145	            unique.append(url)
146	
147	    return unique[:200]  # Cap at 200 L1 URLs
148

150	# ── L2: S2 Citation Graph ────────────────────────────────────────────
151	
152	
153	def _get_seed_papers(academic_results: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
154	    """Get top-cited papers as seeds for citation chasing."""
155	    papers = [
156	        r for r in academic_results
157	        if r.get("citation_count", 0) > 5 or r.get("citationCount", 0) > 5
158	    ]
159	    papers.sort(
160	        key=lambda r: r.get("citation_count", r.get("citationCount", 0)),
161	        reverse=True,
162	    )
163	    return papers[:10]  # Top 10 most-cited

166	async def _get_s2_references(
167	    seed_papers: list[dict],
168	    known_urls: set[str],
169	) -> list[str]:
170	    """Fetch reference lists from S2 for seed papers."""
171	    import aiohttp
172	
173	    urls = []
174	    headers = {}
175	    if S2_API_KEY:
176	        headers["x-api-key"] = S2_API_KEY
177	
178	    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
179	        for paper in seed_papers:
180	            # Get paper ID (S2 ID, DOI, or URL-derived)
181	            paper_id = (
182	                paper.get("paperId")
183	                or paper.get("semantic_scholar_id")
184	                or paper.get("doi")
185	            )
186	            if not paper_id:
187	                continue
188	
189	            try:
190	                api_url = (
191	                    f"https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/{paper_id}"
192	                    f"/references?fields=title,url,openAccessPdf&limit={CRAWL_L2_MAX_REFS}"
193	                )
194	                async with session.get(api_url, headers=headers, timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=15)) as resp:
195	                    if resp.status == 200:
196	                        data = await resp.json()
197	                        for ref in data.get("data", []):
198	                            cited = ref.get("citedPaper", {})
199	                            # Prefer open access PDF URL
200	                            oa = cited.get("openAccessPdf", {})
201	                            url = (oa.get("url") if oa else None) or cited.get("url", "")
202	                            if url and url not in known_urls:
203	                                urls.append(url)
204	
205	                    # Rate limit: 1 RPS for S2
206	                    await asyncio.sleep(1.0)
207	
208	            except Exception as exc:
209	                logger.debug("[deep-crawl] S2 reference fetch failed for %s: %s", paper_id, str(exc)[:80])
210	
211	    return urls[:100]  # Cap at 100 L2 URLs
212	
213	
214	# ── Batch Fetching ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
215	
216	
217	async def _fetch_batch(
218	    urls: list[str],
219	    known_urls: set[str],
220	    deadline: float,
221	) -> list[dict]:
222	    """Fetch URLs concurrently via the existing content fetch pipeline."""
223	    results: list[dict] = []
224	    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(CRAWL_CONCURRENCY)
225	
226	    async def fetch_one(url: str) -> dict | None:
227	        if time.time() >= deadline:
228	            return None
229	        async with semaphore:
230	            return await _fetch_single_url(url)
231	
232	    tasks = [fetch_one(url) for url in urls if url not in known_urls]
233	    if not tasks:
234	        return results
235	
236	    completed = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
237	    for item in completed:
238	        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get("content"):
239	            results.append(item)
240	
241	    return results
242	
243	
244	async def _fetch_single_url(url: str) -> dict | None:
245	    """Fetch a single URL using the existing access bypass pipeline."""
246	    try:
247	        # Try to use existing content cache first
248	        try:
249	            from src.polaris_graph.memory.content_cache import get_cached_content
250	            cached = await get_cached_content(url)
251	            if cached:
252	                return {
253	                    "url": url,
254	                    "content": cached.get("content", ""),
255	                    "title": cached.get("title", ""),
256	                    "source_type": "deep_crawl_cached",
257	                }
258	        except Exception:
259	            pass
260	
261	        # Fetch via Jina Reader (free, fast)
262	        import aiohttp
263	        jina_url = f"https://r.jina.ai/{url}"
264	        headers = {
265	            "Accept": "text/markdown",
266	            "X-Return-Format": "markdown",
267	        }
268	
269	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
270	            async with session.get(
271	                jina_url,
272	                headers=headers,
273	                timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=30),
274	            ) as resp:
275	                if resp.status == 200:
276	                    content = await resp.text()
277	                    if content and len(content) > 100:
278	                        # Cache it
279	                        try:
280	                            from src.polaris_graph.memory.content_cache import cache_content
281	                            await cache_content(url, content, title="", fetch_method="jina_deep_crawl")
282	                        except Exception:
283	                            pass
284	
285	                        return {
286	                            "url": url,
287	                            "content": content[:30000],  # Cap content length
288	                            "title": "",
289	                            "source_type": "deep_crawl",
290	                        }
291	
292	    except Exception as exc:
293	        logger.debug("[deep-crawl] Failed to fetch %s: %s", url[:60], str(exc)[:80])
294	
295	    return None
296
